plan: We're trimming SPY to 20% to meet the concentration mandate and reduce overall exposure.
All leveraged ETFs, SOXL and SPAL, are being sold to zero for capital preservation.
CRWD is trimmed to 10% to reduce single name concentration and align with conservative limits.
We're adding to QQQI, increasing its allocation to 25%, to favor income and stability.
A new position in NOW is initiated at 5% to diversify with a top-quality setup.
These moves reduce leverage and concentration, prioritizing capital preservation.
conservative.read: This conservative plan aims for a target of ~1.5% per month, focusing on capital preservation. Risk is significantly reduced by eliminating leveraged ETFs and lowering concentration. Expect lower volatility and drawdowns.

plan: SPY is trimmed to 25% to align with the concentration limits and reduce overall exposure.
SOXL is sold entirely due to its underperformance and being below its stop.
SPAL is trimmed to 3% to reduce leveraged ETF exposure while maintaining a small allocation.
CRWD is trimmed to 10% to reduce single name concentration.
We're buying ANET for 7% of the book, adding a quality tech name with a strong scanner read.
A new position in TECL is initiated at 5% to add a high-scoring tech ETF for growth.
balanced.read: This balanced plan targets ~2.5% per month, prioritizing risk-first growth. Leverage is reduced, and concentration is managed, blending broad index with quality tech. Expect moderate volatility and drawdowns.

plan: SPY is trimmed to 25% to meet the mandate, freeing capital for higher-conviction plays.
SOXL is sold entirely due to its underperformance and being below its stop, freeing capital.
SPAL is increased to 5% to lean into leveraged growth opportunities.
CRWD is increased to 15% to press a strong performing AI/Software name.
A new position in NOW is initiated at 10%, focusing on the highest-scoring scanner setup.
We're adding TECL at 10% to capitalize on a high-scoring leveraged tech ETF for aggressive growth.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets ~4% per month, aiming for stretched growth. It utilizes leveraged ETFs and concentrates in high-scoring setups. Be aware of significantly higher drawdown potential and the daily decay/rebalancing of leveraged ETFs; strict stop discipline is crucial.

plan: SPY is trimmed to 20% to reduce concentration and free capital for income-focused assets.
QQQI is increased to 30% to significantly boost monthly distributions.
QQQ is trimmed to 10% to reduce growth exposure and shift towards income.
SMH is trimmed to 5% to reduce high-beta sector exposure.
CRWD is sold entirely to exit a high-beta single name.
All leveraged ETFs, SOXL and SPAL, are sold to zero to minimize drawdown risk.
income.read: This income plan targets ~1% per month, prioritizing steady monthly distributions. It focuses on income and broad index ETFs, minimizing leverage and high-beta single names. While aiming for low drawdown, distributions are variable, and NAV can still fall in selloffs.
